roadmap.summary: LifecycleTransition.from always equals .to in NDJSON streaming output. build_lifecycle_event sets from: to_wire (the new state) because dispatch_single never reads the prior observation row before writing. Fix: add prior_state param to build_lifecycle_event; read prior row in all three dispatch arms; regression test that flows through dispatch() directly.

phases.name: Bug fix — prior-state tracking in action shim

steps.name: Regression test: dispatch() produces from != to
criteria: New test s_lt_01_lifecycle_transition_from_reflects_prior_alloc_state added to streaming_submit.rs.
Test calls action_shim::dispatch() directly — NOT emit_lifecycle(). This is the path that exercises build_lifecycle_event.
Test pre-seeds a Running AllocStatusRow for the alloc via write_row() before calling dispatch.
Test subscribes to state.lifecycle_events BEFORE calling dispatch, so the event is not missed.
Test dispatches StopAllocation { alloc_id } via dispatch(vec![Action::StopAllocation { alloc_id }], driver, obs, bus, tick).
Test asserts event.from == AllocStateWire::Running (the prior seeded state).
Test asserts event.to == AllocStateWire::Terminated (the new state after stop).
Test asserts event.from != event.to (the invariant the bug violates).
Test FAILS against current code — both from and to will be Terminated because build_lifecycle_event sets from: to_wire.
RED-phase commit uses --no-verify per testing.md RED scaffolds section; cite this step in commit body.
implementation_notes: TickContext construction — look at TickContext's fields in overdrive_core::reconciler; construct a minimal one with tick: 0 and now: std::time::Instant::now().

The StopAllocation variant is the cleanest choice: it takes only alloc_id (no AllocationSpec), prior_row is already fetched in dispatch_single, and the from=Running → to=Terminated transition is unambiguous.
subscribe() must be called BEFORE dispatch(); otherwise the broadcast event is missed.
Use tokio::sync::broadcast::Sender::subscribe() on state.lifecycle_events, then call recv() on the returned Receiver after dispatch.
Wrap the recv() in tokio::time::timeout(Duration::from_secs(1), ...) so a missed event fails fast.
Do NOT modify the existing tests or helpers — add only the new test function.
This test lives in the default (non-integration-gated) lane — it is in-process with SimDriver and SimObservationStore, no real I/O.

steps.name: Fix: pass prior_state through build_lifecycle_event
criteria: build_lifecycle_event signature changed to fn build_lifecycle_event(row: &AllocStatusRow, prior_state: AllocStateWire, source: TransitionSource) -> LifecycleEvent.
build_lifecycle_event body uses prior_state as the from: field instead of to_wire.
FIXME(slice-02-03) comment removed entirely. The doc comment on build_lifecycle_event updated to describe that from carries the actual prior state.

StartAllocation arm: calls find_prior_alloc_row(obs, &alloc_id).await? before obs.write(). Extracts prior_state = result.map(|r| r.state.into()).unwrap_or(AllocStateWire::Pending). Passes prior_state to build_lifecycle_event.
RestartAllocation arm: extracts let prior_state: AllocStateWire = prior_row.state.into(); before prior_row moves into build_alloc_status_row. Passes prior_state to build_lifecycle_event.
StopAllocation arm: extracts let prior_state: AllocStateWire = prior_row.state.into(); before prior_row moves into build_alloc_status_row. Passes prior_state to build_lifecycle_event.
Regression test s_lt_01_lifecycle_transition_from_reflects_prior_alloc_state from step 01-01 now PASSES.
Full workspace tests pass (cargo nextest run --workspace on macOS --no-run; Lima run for integration-gated tests).
No changes to streaming.rs — the streaming consumer is correct and requires no modification.
Mutation kill rate at least eighty percent on diff scope for action_shim.rs.
implementation_notes: AllocState (the obs-store enum) is distinct from AllocStateWire (the wire enum). Use .into() to convert — the From impl already exists; verify by reading the existing code in dispatch_single where to_wire: AllocStateWire = row.state.into().
find_prior_alloc_row is already defined in the same file and used by RestartAllocation and StopAllocation. Reuse it exactly as the other arms do.

For StartAllocation the alloc may not exist in obs yet (first start). None → AllocStateWire::Pending is the correct sentinel: the allocation conceptually starts in Pending before being started, matching how existing tests model it (make_lifecycle_event passes from: AllocStateWire::Pending).
RestartAllocation already reads prior_row early in the arm. Extract prior_state BEFORE passing prior_row.job_id / prior_row.node_id into build_alloc_status_row to avoid a partial-move compile error.
StopAllocation already reads prior_row. Same extraction pattern as RestartAllocation.
Conventional commit: fix(control-plane): carry prior alloc state in LifecycleEvent.from
Mutation gate: cargo xtask lima run -- cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --features integration-tests --package overdrive-control-plane --file crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/action_shim.rs
